program_outcomes: Mühendislik Bilgisi: Matematik, fen bilimleri, temel mühendislik, bilgisayarla hesaplama ve ilgili mühendislik disiplinine özgü konularda bilgi; bu bilgileri, karmaşık mühendislik problemlerinin çözümünde kullanabilme becerisi.

Problem Analizi: Karmaşık mühendislik problemlerini, temel bilim, matematik ve mühendislik bilgilerini kullanarak ve ele alınan problemle ilgili BM Sürdürülebilir Kalkınma Amaçlarını gözeterek tanımlama, formüle etme ve analiz becerisi.

Mühendislik Tasarımı: Karmaşık mühendislik problemlerine yaratıcı çözümler tasarlama becerisi; karmaşık sistemleri, süreçleri, cihazları veya ürünleri gerçekçi kısıtları ve koşulları gözeterek, mevcut ve gelecekteki gereksinimleri karşılayacak biçimde tasarlama becerisi.

Teknik ve Araçların Kullanımı: Karmaşık mühendislik problemlerinin analizi ve çözümüne yönelik, tahmin ve modelleme de dahil olmak üzere, uygun teknikleri, kaynakları ve modern mühendislik ve bilişim araçlarını, sınırlamalarının da farkında olarak seçme ve kullanma becerisi.

Araştırma ve İnceleme: Karmaşık mühendislik problemlerinin incelenmesi için literatür araştırması, deney tasarlama, deney yapma, veri toplama, sonuçları analiz etme ve yorumlama dahil, araştırma yöntemlerini kullanma becerisi.

Mühendislik Uygulamalarının Küresel Etkisi: Mühendislik uygulamalarının BM Sürdürülebilir Kalkınma Amaçları kapsamında, topluma, sağlık ve güvenliğe, ekonomiye, sürdürülebilirlik ve çevreye etkileri hakkında bilgi; mühendislik çözümlerinin hukuksal sonuçları konusunda farkındalık.

Etik Davranış: Mühendislik meslek ilkelerine uygun davranma, etik sorumluluk hakkında bilgi; hiçbir konuda ayrımcılık yapmadan, tarafsız davranma ve çeşitliliği kapsayıcı olma konularında farkındalık.
Bireysel ve Takım Çalışması: Bireysel olarak ve disiplin içi ve çok disiplinli takımlarda (yüz yüze, uzaktan veya karma) takım üyesi veya lideri olarak etkin biçimde çalışabilme becerisi.
Sözlü ve Yazılı İletişim: Hedef kitlenin çeşitli farklılıklarını (eğitim, dil, meslek gibi) dikkate alarak, teknik konularda sözlü, yazılı etkin iletişim kurma becerisi.
Proje Yönetimi: Proje yönetimi ve ekonomik yapılabilirlik analizi gibi iş hayatındaki uygulamalar hakkında bilgi; girişimcilik ve yenilikçilik hakkında farkındalık.

Yaşam Boyu Öğrenme: Bağımsız ve sürekli öğrenebilme, yeni ve gelişmekte olan teknolojilere uyum sağlayabilme ve teknolojik değişimlerle ilgili sorgulayıcı düşünebilmeyi kapsayan yaşam boyu öğrenme becerisi.

courses.name: İngilizcede Akademik Beceriler I
courses.purpose: This course is a required first-year course designed to develop students' mental skills such as reading, listening, writing, and speaking. It aims to help students use current texts and audio materials to understand text sections, read quickly and effectively, connect ideas across different texts, and use texts as effective sources for learning outcomes. In speaking and writing, students learn to use sources, paraphrase, quote, summarize, and synthesize information, and to write coherent, concise, informative, or persuasive texts that support their ideas.
courses.description: This course aims to enable students to carry out their academic skills in English.
learning_outcomes: Identify connections between ideas in academic texts.
Identify explicitly stated and/or implied opinions in academic texts.
Conduct research using various academic sources.
Summarize an academic source.
Paraphrase an academic opinion.
Quote from an academic source.
Synthesize information obtained from various academic sources.

Hafta 2: Unit I: Communication at University (Sections C-D-E)

courses.name: Fundamentals of Engineering Culture
courses.purpose: The aim of the course is to bring students to a level where they can comprehend the fundamentals of the engineering concept and problem-solving methods in engineering; ethical issues encountered in engineering practices; the environmental, social, and economic sustainability of engineering applications; their impacts on health and safety; how to conduct literature reviews; how to write scientific reports; the fundamentals of entrepreneurial engineering and project management; disaster awareness; the importance of lifelong learning; and the fundamentals of methods for accessing information.
learning_outcomes: Explain the impacts of engineering practices on society, health and safety, economy, sustainability, and the environment within the framework of UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
Explain the legal implications of engineering solutions.
Evaluate engineering activities in terms of adherence to professional principles and ethical responsibilities.
Discuss the responsibilities of engineers in maintaining impartiality and inclusivity regardless of nation, language, religion, race, or gender.
Identify the skills required for entrepreneurship and the steps to establish a business.
Explain the principles of project management and economic feasibility in business practices.
Discuss the stages and principles of conducting a literature review for problem-solving.
Discuss the principles to consider for effective written and oral communication while addressing diverse audiences (e.g., education, language, profession).
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: What is engineering? How do engineers differ from other professions?
Hafta 4: Case studies in engineering ethics
Hafta 5: Sustainability in engineering practices: environmental, social, and economic impacts
Hafta 6: Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and indicators
Hafta 9: Fundamentals of entrepreneurship in engineering
Hafta 12: Using scientific databases and conducting literature reviews; Understanding plagiarism and how to avoid it
resources: Engineering Ethics : An Industrial Perspective; Gail D. Baura; 2006; Elsevier Secience & Technology ISBN: 97801208853
Engineering Entrepreneurship: From Idea to Business Plan Paul Swamidass Cambridge Press ISBN: 978-1-107-65164-7
Sustainable Engineering: Drivers Metrics Tools and Applications Krishna R. Reddy Claudio Cameselle Jeffrey A. Adams ISBN: 978-1-119-49393-8
Software Project Management A Real World Guide to Success Joel Henry 2004 ISBN 032122342X

courses.name: Orientation and Career Planning
courses.purpose: This course aims to prepare students for university education and campus life, help them develop the academic, social, and personal skills they will need throughout their university studies, and guide them in preparing for their professional careers.
courses.description: This course helps students recognize the skills needed for university life and career goals. These skills include self-awareness, goal setting, time management, effective communication, mindfulness, and analytical thinking. The course also raises students' awareness of habits necessary for physical, mental, and spiritual health, as well as issues such as addiction and bullying.
learning_outcomes: Use the opportunities provided by the university's academic and administrative units.
Explain the benefits of acquiring academic skills such as note-taking, academic reading, accessing information, time management, and analytical thinking.
Define social skills such as effective communication and entrepreneurship.
Identify their strengths and weaknesses by using strategies such as goal setting, self-awareness, and mindfulness.
Explain the habits they should adopt and the behaviors they should avoid for their physical, mental, and spiritual health.
Outline the steps required to prepare for career planning.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction: Course Purpose and Scope; University Life: Academic Units, Administrative Units
Hafta 2: Academic Skills: Learning to Learn
Hafta 4: Academic Skills: Note-Taking and Academic Reading
Hafta 5: Academic Skills: Accessing Information and Academic Integrity
Hafta 6: Personal Skills: Effective Time Management and Analytical Thinking
Hafta 7: Personal Skills: Goal Setting and Self-Awareness
Hafta 9: Personal Skills: Effective Communication and Entrepreneurship
Hafta 10: Career Planning: CV Writing and Job Interviews
Hafta 11: Career Planning: Experiences, Success and Failure Stories
Hafta 12: Healthy Living: Sleep, Nutrition, Physical Activity
Hafta 13: Healthy Living: Addiction, Mindfulness, and Bullying in Relationships
resources: https://uzaktanegitimkapisi.cbiko.gov.tr/Egitimler/
https://ytnk.tv/

courses.name: Calculus I
courses.purpose: This course aims to built fundamentals of calculus and its applications for engineers.
courses.description: Calculus I provides important tools in understanding functions of one variable and has led to the development of new areas of mathematics.
learning_outcomes: Will be able to calculate the derivatives of explicit and implicit functions.
Will be able to examine the continuity of functions.
Will be able to calculate the derivatives of explicit and implicit functions.
Will be able to solve related application problems.
Will be able to classify the critical points of functions.
Will be able to plot the graphs of functions.
Will be able to solve extreme value problems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Graphs of quadratic functions, Polynomials and rational functions, the trigonometric functions, examples of velocity, growth rate and area
Hafta 2: Limits of Functions, limits at infinity and infinite limits
Hafta 3: Continuity, tangent lines and their slopes
Hafta 4: The derivative, differentiation rules, the chain rule, derivatives of trigonometric functions
Hafta 5: Higher-order derivatives, the mean value theorem
Hafta 6: Implicit differentiation, inverse functions, Exponential and logarithmic functions
Hafta 7: The natural logarithm and exponential. The inverse trigonometric functions
Hafta 10: Extreme values, concavity and inflections
Hafta 11: Sketching the graph of a function, extreme value problems to improve reading comprehension skills
Hafta 12: Extreme value problems to improve reading comprehension skills, properties of the definite integral. The fundamental theorem of calculus
Hafta 14: The method of substitution, areas of plane regions

''Calculus Early Transcendentals'' James Stewart Cengage Learning; 7th edition 2010.ISBN-13:978-0538497909

courses.name: Academic Skills in English II
courses.purpose: This course aims to enable students to carry out their academic skills in English.
courses.description: ENG 102 is a compulsory first-year course designed to improve mental skills such as reading, listening, writing, and speaking. Students develop academic listening by identifying important and relevant information in lectures or discussions, and reading skills by reading current academic texts and using that information to produce learning outcomes. Speaking focuses on academic presentations, helping students express their thoughts and ideas in a coherent and persuasive way. Writing is mainly intended to reinforce speaking activities.
learning_outcomes: Identify explicitly stated and/or implied views in academic texts
Identify connections between ideas in academic texts
Conduct research using various academic sources
Summarize an academic source
Paraphrase an academic view
Cite from an academic source
Synthesize information obtained from various academic sources

courses.name: Calculus II
courses.purpose: This course examines integration techniques and applications of integration, Taylor and Maclaurin series and their applications, and functions of several variables with their derivatives, integrals, and applications.
courses.description: This course aims to provide information about integration techniques and applications, define functions of several variables, partial differentiation and multiple integration.
learning_outcomes: Evaluate definite and indefinite integrals of functions using integration techniques
Calculate improper integrals and volumes of solids.
Use the applications of Taylor and Maclaurin series effectively.
Define the concepts of limits and continuity for the functions of several variables.
Calculate partial and directional derivatives.
Solve extreme value problems.
Compute double and triple integrals
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Integration by parts, integrals of rational functions
Hafta 3: Integrals of rational functions, inverse substitutions
Hafta 6: Taylor and Maclaurin series, applications of Taylor and Maclaurin series, Functions of several variables
Hafta 8: Partial derivatives, Gradients and directional derivatives
Hafta 10: Gradients and directional derivatives, Extreme values
Hafta 11: Extreme values, Extreme values of functions defined on restricted domains
Hafta 12: Extreme values of functions defined on restricted domains, Lagrange multipliers
Hafta 13: Iteration of double integrals in cartesian coordinates, double integrals in polar coordinates
Hafta 14: Triple integrals. Change of variables in triple integrals

courses.name: Introduction to Programming II
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide the students with general principles underlying the practice of object-oriented programming using Java programming language. In this course, the students will learn the main concepts of object-oriented programming including classes, objects, data members, methods, inheritance, and polymorphism. Object-oriented programming techniques such as implementation of inheritance and polymorphism using abstract classes will also be covered.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamental concepts of object-oriented programming using Java programming language.
learning_outcomes: Design software blueprints using inheritance, polymorphism, and abstraction.
Utilize appropriate memory-resident structures and generic types for efficient data management.
Use lambdas and nested/packaged logical units.
Implement robust Java input/output and text-processing solutions with modern I/O and exception handling.
Comprehend pragmatic programming principles to mitigate common pitfalls.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Object Oriented Programming and Programming Paradigms
Hafta 2: Thinking in Objects (class definition, constructor, getter setter, etc. review)
Hafta 9: Object Oriented Software Architectures, Java Packages and Nested Classes
Hafta 12: Modern Java IO I (Streams, Buffers, Serialization)
Hafta 13: Modern Java IO II (Text Processing)
resources: Java How to Program 10/e (Early Objects) Global Edition Paul Deitel and Harvey Deitel Pearson ISBN13: 9781292018195

courses.name: Data Structures and Algorithms I
courses.purpose: The objective of this course is to teach students the notion of an abstract data type (ADT) which is central to the design and analysis of computer algorithms. This course introduces abstract data types, and presents algorithms and data structures for implementing several ADTs. It emphasizes the efficiency of algorithms as evaluated by asymptotic analysis of running time. The programming assignments will be given in the programming languages taught in SE 115 and/or SE116.
courses.description: Algorithm analysis, linear data structures, trees, hashing, priority queues, sorting, and graph algorithms.
learning_outcomes: will be able to analyze the loop structures of either recursive or non-recursive algorithms to express their asymptotic running times using big-Oh notation.

will be able to assess the relative advantages of using array or linked list implementations versus hashing in efficiently solving search problems with concurrent insertion, and/or deletions on collections of data.

will be able to develop efficient computer programs running in O (log n) per searching, insertion and/or deletion of data items by employing correct variants of tree data structures covered in the course.
will be able to select the right sorting algorithm for efficient applications requiring an order on data items.
will be able to describe the usage of various data structures.
will be able to explain the operations for maintaining common data structures.
will be able to devise appropriate data structures for solving specific computing problems.
will be able to use various graph algorithms to design solutions to simple computing problems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction: Mathematics Review and Recursion
Hafta 2: Algorithm Analysis (basic concepts of algorithms, modeling runtimes, recurrences, Big-Oh notations, Running Time Calculations)
Hafta 3: Algorithm Analysis and Linear Data Structures: (Linked Lists)
Hafta 4: Linear Data Structures (Linked Lists, Stacks, Stack Applications)
Hafta 5: Linear Data Structures (Queues) and Trees (Binary trees)
Hafta 13: Graph Algorithms (Definitions, Representation, Topological Sort)
resources: M. A. Weiss. Data Structures and Algorithm Analysis in Java. 3/e. Pearson. 2012. 978-0132576277
R. Sedgewick. K. Wayne. Algorithms. 4/e. Addison-Wesley Professional. 2011. 978-0321573513.

courses.name: Atatürk İlkeleri ve İnkılap Tarihi
courses.purpose: Atatürk İlkeleri ve İnkılâp Tarihi Dersinin amacı, üniversite gençliğine Türkiye Cumhuriyeti’nin hangi koşullarda kurulduğunu anlatmak, Atatürkçü Düşünce’yi ve onun dayandığı felsefeyi, ilkeleri ve bunun yanında çağdaş değerleri benimsetmektir.
courses.description: Dersin içeriği, Atatürk önderliğinde Türk milletinin geçirdiği inkılap tarihini kapsamaktadır. Osmanlı Devleti'nin yıkılış sebepleri irdelenerek, fiilen yok olmuş bir devletten Türkiye Cumhuriyeti Devleti'nin nasıl kurulduğu belgeler ışığında değerlendirilmektedir. İlke ve inkılapların anlamı ve önemi üzerinde durularak yakın dönem tarihimiz aydınlatılmaya çalışılmaktadır. Geçmişten günümüze küresel sorunlar ve olası çözümleri tartışılmaktadır.
learning_outcomes: Tarihi belirleyen çesitli aktörleri açıklayabilecektir.
Bagımsızlık, Özgürlük ve Ulusal Egemenlik kavramlarını yorumlayabilecektir.
Çagdas, Demokratik, Lâik ve Sosyal bir Hukuk Devleti olan Türkiye Cumhuriyeti'nin olusum sürecini degerlendirebilecektir.
Milli birlik ve beraberliğin önemini kavrayabilecektir.
Türkiye Cumhuriyeti Devleti'nin ulusal hedef ve amaçlarını açıklayabilecektir.
Türk toplumunun karşılaşacağı sorunları Atatürkçü bir yaklaşımla çözebilecektir.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Atatürk İlkeleri ve İnkılâp Tarihi Dersinin Amacı, İnkılap ve İnkılap İle İlgili Kavramlar
Hafta 2: Osmanlı Devleti’nin Yıkılışı ve Osmanlı Devleti’nde Yenilik Hareketleri
Hafta 4: 1. Dünya Savaşı ve Osmanlı Devleti’nin Yıkılışı
Hafta 7: TBMM'nin Açılması ve Çalışmaları/ TBMM’nin Açılmasından Sonraki Siyasi ve Askeri Gelişmeler
Hafta 9: Lozan Barış Antlaşması ve Siyasi İnkılaplar
Hafta 10: Atatürk İnkılapları (Hukuk Alanı, Eğitim ve Kültür Alanı, Toplumsal Alan, Ekonomik Alan) Atatürk İlkeleri ve Bütünleyici İlkeler
Hafta 11: Atatürk İnkılapları(Hukuk Alanı, Eğitim ve Kültür Alanı, Toplumsal Alan, Ekonomik Alan) Atatürk İlkeleri ve Bütünleyici İlkeler
resources: Hasan Mert, Nuri Karakaş, vd., Türkiye Cumhuriyeti Tarihi, Editör: Elçin Yılmaz, İzmir Ekonomi Üniversitesi Yayınları, 2024.
Mustafa Kemal Atatürk, Nutuk, Atatürk Araştırma Merkezi, Ankara 2005
Bernard Lewis, Modern Türkiye’nin Doğuşu, Ankara, 2017
Lord Kinross, Atatürk, İstanbul, 1981
Mahmut Goloğlu, Devrimler ve Tepkileri, İstanbul, 2009

courses.name: Introduction to Differential Equations I
courses.purpose: This course is an introduction to the basic concepts, theory, methods and applications of ordinary differential equations. The aim of this course is to solve differential equations and to develop the basics of modeling of real life problems.
courses.description: In this course basic concepts of differential equations will be discussed. The types of first order ordinary differential equations will be given and the solution methods will be taught. Also, solution methods for higher order ordinary differential equations will be analyzed.
learning_outcomes: Will be able to use the Laplace transform method to solve linear ordinary differential equations.
Will be able to find solutions of systems of linear diffrential equations.
will be able to find solutions of homogeneous and nonhomogeneous second order linear differential equations.
will be able to solve first order ordinary differential equations and interpret their qualitative behaviour.
will be able to define and classify differential equations, and establish the relationship between the initial value and the existence interval of the solution.
Will be able to apply mathematical modelling in areas such as physics, engineering, biology or economics and interpret their solutions.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction, classification of differential equations, mathematical modeling, and the fundamentals of ecological mathematical models.
Hafta 2: Separable Differential Equations. First Order Linear Differential Equations.
Hafta 3: R. Kent Nagle, Edward B. Saff and Arthur David Snider, ''Fundamentals of Differential Equations and Boundary Value Problems'’, (Pearson, 2011), Section: 2.2, 2.3
Hafta 4: Bernoulli Differential Equations. Existence and uniqueness theorem.
Hafta 5: Homogeneous, Non-homogeneous Constant Coefficient Second Order Differential Equations. The Method of Undetermined Coefficients.
Hafta 6: Non-homogeneous Constant Coefficient Second Order Differential Equations. The Method of Undetermined Coefficients. Variation of parameters.
Hafta 7: Homogeneous, Non-homogeneous Variable Coefficient Second Order Differential Equations.
Hafta 8: Higher order linear equations: General theory, systems of linear differential equations, and distinct eigenvalues, with applications in ecosystem modeling and biodiversity dynamics.
Hafta 10: Systems of Linear Differential Equations, Distinct eigenvalues and Complex eigenvalues.
Hafta 11: Systems of Linear Differential Equations, Complex and repeated eigenvalues.
Hafta 12: Laplace Transforms: Definition of the Laplace Transform, Inverse Laplace Transforms
Hafta 13: Solving Initial Value Problems by Laplace Transforms. Laplace transforms of discontinuous functions: Unit step functions, pulse functions and impulse functions.

Hafta 14: Laplace transforms of discontinuous functions: Unit step functions, pulse functions and impulse functions. Convolution Integral, Convolution theorem. Solutions of integro differential equations.
resources: Kent Nagle, Edward B. Saff and Arthur David Snider, “Fundamentals of Differential Equations and Boundary Value Problems” 6th Edition (Pearson, 2011)

courses.name: Database Systems
courses.purpose: The goal of this course is to give basic knowledge of database systems to a student who intends to be a computer or software engineer. It provides a comprehensive introduction to relational data model and entity relationship data model as a design tool. Functional and multivalued dependencies in the context of normalization process are described in detail for designing relational database schema. SQL database language and system aspects of SQL such as transaction management, indexing, constraints, triggers and authorization are studied in detail together with laboratory practices illustrating different ways of database programming.
courses.description: Topics related to both database design and database programming are covered.
learning_outcomes: Identify and define all the information needed in the design of a database schema specific to a database application.
Create conceptual and physical database designs for a management information system by drawing the E/R diagram and performing the normalization of relations.
Understand the core terms, concepts, and tools of relational database management systems.
Query a database using SQL.
Implement database applications by properly managing concurrent transactions.

Finetune a database design to improve the performance of applications by the use of beneficial indexes and additional data structures conforming to the characteristics of applications running on them.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Database Systems, Relational Data Model, Semi Structured, Data Model
Hafta 3: Introduction to Relational Databases, Functional Dependencies
Hafta 4: Design of Relational Databases, Multivalued Dependencies
Hafta 5: Functional Dependencies and Multivalued Dependencies Revisited
Hafta 10: SQL Programming: Embedded SQL, PSM (PL/SQL)
Hafta 11: SQL Programming: CLI, JDBC, PHP/PEAR
Hafta 14: Logical Query Languages (Datalog) and SQL Recursion
resources: J. D. Ullman. J. Widom. A First Course In Database Systems. 3/e. PrenticeHall. 2008 Course slides (URL for textbook: http://wwwdb.stanford.edu/~ullman/fcdb.html)
Silberschatz et. al. Database System Concepts. 4th ed. McGrawHill. 2002.

courses.name: Technical English for Engineers
courses.purpose: This course aims to improve students’ ability to understand and write technical instructions. It also develops the fundamentals of technical report writing and helps students focus on written and oral professional communication within a disciplinary framework.
courses.description: This course develops technical writing and speaking skills by involving students in project-based activities, with emphasis on using language appropriate to technical contexts, technical report writing, summarizing, and preparing project proposals.
learning_outcomes: Create different types of engineering documents for both technical and non-technical audiences with appropriate structure, grammar, and vocabulary.
Give clear and understandable technical instructions.
Follow technical instructions.
Cite sources ethically to support a logical argument.
Communicate effectively across disciplines in the field of engineering.
Prepare a scientific document on a technical topic as part of a team.
Deliver a presentation on a scientific topic using appropriate terminology.
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Basic Rules of Technical Writing
Hafta 12: The Importance of Effective Instructions
Hafta 14: Project Final Presentation and Final Evaluation
Hafta 15: Getting a Job as an Engineer
resources: McArthur Yalçuk, P. Axis: Technical English for Engineers

courses.name: Probability for Engineers
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the theory of probability and its applications to engineering problems.
courses.description: In this course some important theorems about probability are investigated. In addition, applications of random variables and their probability distributions are discussed.
learning_outcomes: Find mean and variance of random variables.
Examine the relationship between two random variables.
Apply discrete and continuous distributions.
Investigate the advantages of joint probability distributions.
Use conditional probability, the total probability rule and Bayes’ theorem. Compute discrete and continuous random variables.
Use fundamental concepts such as sample space, events and counting techniques. Explain concepts of probability.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Events and counting sample points
Hafta 3: Counting sample points, probability of an event and additive rules
Hafta 4: Additive rules, conditional probability of an event
Hafta 6: Concept of random variable and discrete random variable
Hafta 7: Discrete probability distributions, expected value and variance of discrete random variable
Hafta 8: Uniform, Binomial, Negative Binomial, Hypergeometric, Poisson distributions (vaccine efficacy, epidemics, communicable diseases, traffic accidents, environmental pollution)
Hafta 10: Continuous probability distributions, expected value and variance of continuous random variable
Hafta 11: Uniform, Normal, areas under the normal curve, applications of the normal dist. and exponential distribution
Hafta 13: Joint probability distributions, variance and covariance
Hafta 14: Multinomial distributions, linear functions of random variables, moment-generating functions

courses.name: Linear Algebra for Engineers
courses.purpose: The main objective of this course is to establish a basic mathematical background for the students who will receive engineering courses based on linear algebra by providing them with the basic knowledge on linear vector spaces, matrix operations as well as on the methods for solving and analyzing linear systems of algebraic equations.
courses.description: The main subjects of the course are the vector and matrix operations, linear independence and dependence of vectors, linear vector spaces and subspaces, dimensions and basis vectors for vector spaces, linear transformations, determinants, eigenvalue and eigenvectors.
learning_outcomes: apply the row operations to find (reduced) row echelon forms of matrices.
find the inverse of a matrix.
apply basic concepts of linear models to various applications.
evaluate the determinants of matrices.
investigate the linear independence of vectors.
identify vector spaces and their subspaces.
compute the eigenvalues of a matrix and corresponding eigenvectors.
describe the inner product.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Systems of linear equations, row reduction and echelon forms, vector equations
Hafta 2: The matrix equation Ax=b, Solution sets of linear systems, applications of linear systems
Hafta 3: Linear Independence, introduction to linear transformations
Hafta 4: The matrix of a linear transformation and applied modeling of electrical networks, population movement, and infrastructure flows
Hafta 5: Matrix operations, The inverse of a matrix
Hafta 6: Characterization of invertible matrices, matrix factorizations and decomposition learning
Hafta 7: Introduction to determinants, properties of determinants
Hafta 8: Cramer’s rule, volume, and linear transformations, Vector spaces and subspaces
Hafta 10: Null spaces, column spaces, and linear transformations, Linearly independent sets, bases
Hafta 11: The dimension of a vector space, Rank, Application for Markov chains
Hafta 12: Eigenvalues and eigenvectors, The characteristic equation, Diagonalization
Hafta 13: Diagonalization, Inner product, length, and orthogonality, orthogonal sets

courses.name: Software Specification and Design
courses.purpose: The purpose of this course is to identify user requirements for complex systems and design an information system. This course covers data collection, analysis, prioritization, balancing, and modeling, and provides students with skills in these areas. This course aims to provide comprehensive knowledge and skills in existing system literature review, software requirement documentation, industrial standards, and UML models. Each student is required to prepare a project to demonstrate the skills they have developed in this course.
courses.description: This course introduces students to the specification and design phases of software engineering. In addition to this theoretical knowledge, students are provided with the opportunity to practice their theoretical knowledge by conducting a project with real users.
learning_outcomes: To have knowledge about software requirements specification and software design.
To conduct a literature study and gather information about existing and similar information systems.
To model the requirements and transform them into design.
To collect data from users and analyze the results.
To use techniques and tools related to software development and design.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to System Analysis and Design
Hafta 2: Software life cycle and project management.
Hafta 3: Requirements determination and data collection
Hafta 4: Business Process and Functional Modeling
Hafta 5: User scenario analysis and design
resources: Dennis A. Wixom B.H. and Tegarden D. Systems Analysis and Design: An Object Oriented Approach with UML 5th ed. Wiley 2015 ISBN: 978-1-118-80467-4.

courses.name: Concepts of Programming Languages
courses.purpose: This course provides students with a wide-range in-depth discussion of programming language concepts. By examining design issues for various language constructs, the design choices made in common languages, and the alternatives among those choices, it builds a solid foundation for understanding the fundamental concepts of programming languages.
courses.description: The course covers phases of compilation, including lexical and syntax analysis, code optimization, and code generation; names, bindings, type checking, scopes, data types, expressions, assignment statements, subprograms and their implementation, abstract data types and encapsulation constructs, support for object-oriented programming, exception handling, and event handling.
learning_outcomes: identify various principles and paradigms in programming languages
recognize grammars used in lexical and syntax analysis
discuss issues in language design including types, binding, lifetime, storage, scope, expressions, control structures and functions
explain the conceptual basis of object-oriented programming languages
develop small programs using various programming languages
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Preliminaries, language categories, evaluation implementation methods, compilation phases (code optimization and code generation)
Hafta 2: Describing Syntax and Semantics, Lexical and Syntax analysis
Hafta 3: Names, bindings, type checking, and scopes
Hafta 4: Data types: Primitive, Enumeration, Arrays, Records, Unions
Hafta 5: Data types: Pointers, Reference, Garbage Collection, Type checking and equivalence
Hafta 7: Subprograms: Fundamentals, Parameter passing, Design issues for subroutines
Hafta 10: Implementing subprograms: program layout in the memory, Stack and heap memory management (ARI, frames), register allocation for compiled programs
Hafta 11: Abstract data types and encapsulation constructs
Hafta 14: Exception handling and event handling
resources: Concepts of Programming Languages. International Edition 11th Edition by Roberto Sebesta (2016)

courses.name: Engineering Statistics
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with the skills to collect, analyze and interpret statistical data.
courses.description: This course focuses on sampling distributions, statistical estimation, hypothesis testing, simple and multiple linear regression. In addition, experimental design and applications of these methods to industrial systems engineering are discussed.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Statistics and Data Analysis and Business
Hafta 2: Introduction to Statistics and Data Analysis and Business, Data Description
Hafta 3: Fundamental Sampling Distributions and Methods of Point Estimation
Hafta 4: Fundamental Sampling Distributions and Methods of Point Estimation
Hafta 11: Two-Sample Tests of Hypotheses and Two-Sample Tests of Hypotheses. Applications in public health, healthcare or financial markets
Hafta 13: Hypothesis Tests in Simple Linear Regression, Confidence Intervals

courses.name: Software Architecture
courses.purpose: The goal of this course to arm the students with the knowledge needed in architecting effective and maintainable complex software systems of high quality by applying design patterns. Each pattern represents a best practice solution to a software problem in some context. The course will sensitize the student that there is rarely one "right" design and an engineer is faced with a spectrum of possibilities representing tradeoffs. The course will cover the rationale and benefits of design patterns in architecting software systems. The course includes a brief review of object-oriented design principles and UML. Programming assignments and a project in the Java language will provide experience in the use of these patterns.
courses.description: This course covers the principals behind the software design patterns and their application in constructing software components.
learning_outcomes: be able to state the intention of the pattern and show in UML notation.
be able to identify the participants and their responsibilities
be able to contrast the difference in intentions between structurally similar patterns
be able to apply several appropriate patterns in the implementation of software design problem
be able to select appropriate design patterns to improve an existing design
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: A refresher on Object Oriented Design and UML. Architectural Principles.
Hafta 6: Factory and Abstract Factory (Creational) Patterns
resources: "Design patterns: Elements of Reusable Object Oriented Software” by E. Gamma R. Helm R. Johnson and J. Vlissides. Addison Wesley. 1995. ISBN: 978-0-201-63361-0. Instructor Notes
Metseker and Wake “Design Patterns in Java” Addison Wesley 2006.

courses.name: Software Verification and Validation
courses.purpose: The first objective of this course is to provide students with information about the fundamentals and principles of software testing. The second objective is to provide students with information about basic testing methods and software quality in order to develop high-quality and complex software products. The third objective of the course is to provide students with the necessary skills in software testing in the international software testing certification process. These objectives will be achieved by the student learning software testing processes, test documentation, testing techniques, test management, test tools, test data collection, test result analysis and reporting, the concept of quality in writing, and developing skills.
courses.description: One of the generally accepted issues in software development is that it is not possible to develop perfect software. Therefore, before the software is put into use, it is necessary to eliminate existing errors and reduce the possible effects of incorrect operation. This is especially important for complex and security-critical software systems. In this course, testing processes in software engineering, test documentation, concepts related to testing techniques and software quality are taught and the aim is for the student to gain skills.
learning_outcomes: To have information about software testing, quality, and standards.
To develop skills in software testing techniques, tools, and models.
To design and analyze test cases by creating software test scenarios, test sets, and test input data.
To manage, plan, and prepare a test project by applying the mission, goals, and processes determined for testing.
To define quality control and quality assurance approaches using software testing knowledge.
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Testing throughout the software development life cycle
Hafta 13: Case study for complex systems
Hafta 14: Case study for complex systems
Hafta 15: Case study for complex systems

courses.name: System Programming
courses.purpose: The goal of this course is to extend students’ knowledge in systems programming by focusing on practical network and multi threaded programming. Topics such as synchronization primitives, deadlocks, communication protocols, socket programming are introduced in this course. Operating system concepts that support these topics will be discussed. The students will gain familiarity on information security, authentication, confidentiality, integrity and digital signing at a beginning level. The Java programming language will be used throughout the course. All laboratory exercises and assignments will be performed in Java; therefore, to be able to complete this course, a student should have good programming skills in Java.
courses.description: To acquaint students with basic knowledge to develop systems programs that involves multi-threading and computer networks. The course provides an introduction to basic operating system concepts, multi-threading, socket programming and information security.
learning_outcomes: be able to explain user/kernel execution modes, system calls, processor interrupts and context switching
be able to explain process and threading concepts, advantages and potential problems of thread and process based programming,
be able to design experiments to illustrate advantages obtained from threading and concurrency,
be able to implement socket programming and multi-threaded networking program,
be able to implement socket programming and multi-threaded networking program
be able to apply security concepts of authentication, confidentiality, integrity and digital signing
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to basic Operating System concepts
Hafta 6: Introduction to Networking Basics-Network Hardware and Software
Hafta 11: Security Basics. General overview and definitions of information security.
Hafta 12: Practical information security: authentication, hashing, symmetric and asymmetric encryption and decryption techniques, digital signature.

courses.name: Turkish Language
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı; öğrencilere sözlü ve yazılı olarak anlama ve anlatma yeteneğiyle ilgili temel becerileri kazandırmak, edebi metinleri okuma, anlama ve değerlendirme yetkinliği kazandırmak ve proje ya da sunum hazırlarken kullanabilecekleri yöntemleri öğretmektir. Ayrıca öğrencilerin yazılı anlatım biçimleri konusunda gelişmeleri ve çevrim içi ortamda düşüncelerini etkili biçimde ifade edebilmeleri hedeflenir.
courses.description: Bu derste öğrencilerin yazılı ve sözlü iletişim araçlarını etkili ve doğru şekilde kullanması üzerinde durulur. Anlama, anlatma, okuma ve yazma etkinlikleri kapsamında çeşitli sözlü ve yazılı anlatım türleri incelenir; noktalama işaretleri, yazım kuralları, kompozisyon, resmi yazılar, düşünce ve bilgi aktaran yazılar, sözlü anlatım türleri ve bilimsel araştırma ile etkili sunum teknikleri ele alınır.
learning_outcomes: Sözlü ve yazılı anlatımın kurallarını kavrar, düşüncelerini etkili bir biçimde ifade etme becerisi kazanır.
Konuşmanın oluşumunu ve konuşmayı bozan etkenleri kavrar.
Cümleleri oluşturan sözcüklerin ve söz diziminin önemini fark eder.
Okuma alışkanlığı kazanır ve okuduğu kitabı veya katıldığı etkinliği kendi bakış açısıyla yorumlar.
Gelecek yaşamına yönelik olarak dilekçe, tutanak, mektup, öz geçmiş ve sunum yöntemleri gibi yazılı ve sözlü iletişim araçlarını kullanır.

Hafta 3: Yeryüzündeki Diller ve Türkçenin Dünya Dilleri Arasındaki Yeri
Hafta 4: Kültür ve Kültüre Ait Unsurlar
Hafta 5: Yazım Kuralları (Düzeltme işareti, Bazı Sözcük ve Eklerin Yazılışı, Küçük Harflerin Kullanıldığı Yerler, Büyük Harflerin Kullanıldığı Yerler)
Hafta 6: Yazım Kuralları (Birleşik Kelimelerin Yazılışı, Deyimlerin Yazılışı, İkilemelerin Yazılışı, Alinti Sözcüklerin Yazılışı, Yabancı Özel Adların Yazılışı)

courses.name: Multidisciplinary Engineering Projects
courses.purpose: The aim of the course is for students to work on an interdisciplinary project in groups of at least five people, consisting of students from at least three different departments, using the knowledge they have acquired up to the fourth year. They are expected to develop a solution proposal, prepare a report explaining the project process, and present this report orally.
courses.description: The student designs a project as part of a multidisciplinary team using the engineering techniques they have learned, performs its analysis, and prepares business plans. They are able to report the project details and present them using contemporary tools.
learning_outcomes: Conduct literature research to analyze complex engineering problems
Work effectively as a member or leader in multidisciplinary teams (face-to-face, remote, or hybrid).
Prepare a technical project report considering the diverse characteristics of the target audience (education, language, profession).
Deliver an oral presentation of a technical project report considering the diverse characteristics of the target audience..
Independently and continuously learn, adapt to emerging technologies, and select the most accurate information sources while thinking critically about technological changes.
Apply the planning, analysis, development, implementation, and improvement stages of project management through a real project.
Conduct project feasibility assessment
weekly_topics: Hafta 9: Practical teamwork on project implementation
Hafta 10: Practical teamwork on project implementation
Hafta 11: Practical teamwork on project implementation
Hafta 12: Practical teamwork on project implementation
resources: Graduation Project Guide (available at: http://fecs.ieu.edu.tr/en)

courses.name: Operating Systems Security
courses.purpose: The objective of this course is to teach the students security related details of most widely used operating systems, threat analysis, and countermeasures against the threats.
courses.description: A study of technical security policies, models, and mechanisms for confidentiality, integrity, and availability with respect to operating systems from an engineering point of view.
learning_outcomes: classify the security-critical sections of operating systems
specify methods needed to analyze and discover threats against operating systems
identify techniques and tools needed to implement countermeasures against threats
apply different protection mechanisms
analyze the security of a system
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction, basic terms & concepts in information security & related legal issues
Hafta 2: Basics of OS Security; concepts, general & common problems, threats, countermeasures
Hafta 3: Access controls & methodologies, IAAA in OS & relevant technologies & applications in today’s world
Hafta 4: Centralized / decentralized IAAA solutions, different IAAA architectures in secure OS
Hafta 6: Intro to cryptography, protection of assets, data, systems in OS with today’s best practices
Hafta 7: Secure system management; models, solutions, various technologies
Hafta 9: Open source architecture & approach and its relation with information security & OS security
Hafta 10: IAAA differences of Linux vs. Windows architectures
Hafta 11: IAAA differences of Linux vs. Windows architectures
Hafta 12: Network related issues in OS Security
Hafta 13: Network related issues in OS Security (including cryptographic basics)
Hafta 14: Related vulnerabilities, threats & countermeasures (DOS attacks, malicious codes, XSS, rootkits, JavaScript, SQL, Ajax, .Net, Apache, IIS, etc)

courses.name: Automata Theory and Formal Languages
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to introduce automata theory and formal languages, which are one step more abstract than existing programming languages. Fundamental models of computation that form the basis for various branches of computer science, such as compiler design and software engineering, will be presented. At the end of the course, all students are expected to have mastered all these concepts from an engineering perspective.
courses.description: In this course, the most fundamental theories of computer science will be covered, including regular expressions and context-free languages, finite and pushdown automata, Turing machines, computability, undecidability and problem complexity.
learning_outcomes: Will be able to map the Chomsky language hierarchy to the corresponding automata and grammar types,
Will be able to trace a given automata or grammar,
Will be able to translate a given automaton or grammar into another equivalent form,
Design an automata or grammar for a given language,
Will be able to use LR(k) parsers for efficient processing of context-insensitive languages,
Will be able to define the concepts of polynomial time, non-deterministic polynomial time, NP-completeness, decidability and undecidability.
weekly_topics: Hafta 13: Complexity classes P, NP, and NP complete
resources: Introduction to the theory of computation. 3rd Edition. Michael Sipser. ISBN 113318779X.
https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/18-404j-theory-of-computation-fall-2020/

courses.name: Pattern Recognition
courses.purpose: The course focuses on the theory and applications of pattern recognition. It introduces the main ideas and methods used to solve pattern classification problems.
courses.description: Learning and adoption, Bayesian decision theory, discriminant functions, parametric techniques, maximum likelihood estimation, Bayesian estimation, sufficient statistics, non-parametric techniques, linear discriminants, algorithm independent machine learning, classifiers, unsupervised learning, clustering.
learning_outcomes: will be able to design basic and advanced pattern recognition systems.
will be able to explain main approaches in statistical and syntactic pattern recognition.
will be able to describe main issues involved in pattern recognition system design.
will be able to compare different pattern recognition techniques.
will be able to apply pattern recognition techniques using computer toolboxes.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Pattern Recognition, Learning and Adoption
Hafta 4: Parametric Techniques: Maximum Likelihood Estimation and Bayesian Estimation, Sufficient Statistics
resources: Duda. R.O.Hart. P.E. and Stork. D.G. Pattern Classification. Wiley-Interscience. 2nd Edition. 2001.

Marsland. S. Machine Learning: An Algorithmic Perspective. CRC Press. 2009. (Also uses Python.)
Theodoridis. S. and Koutroumbas. K. Pattern Recognition. Edition 4. Academic Press. 2008.

courses.name: Computer Networks
courses.purpose: The goal of this course is to familiarize students with the concepts of data communication, computer networks, and Internetworking. At the end of this course, students will be able to understand the principles of computer networking, including protocol features, protocol layering, addressing, routing, and basic network security issues. Students will be able to enumerate the architectural structures of the ISO/OSI and TCP/IP and explain functions of each layer. TCP/IP layers and their network traffic will be analyzed using dedicated tools such as TCPDUMP and Wireshark. Client-server programs will be developed by using Java socket library.
courses.description: Alongside each layer and protocols of computer networks, the following topics will also be discussed: OSI model of network; MAC protocol; TCP and UDP protocols; error control, detection and correction; IPv4; routing; socket programming; network security.
learning_outcomes: describe the structures and functionalities of the data link and MAC layers
discuss the concept of network layer and the design of local area networks (LAN)
express the working principles of the transport layer protocols (TCP and UDP)
monitor the network traffic using packet analysis tools such as Wireshark and TCPDUMP
implement client-server applications using Java socket programming library
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Computer Networks: Tools, techniques and methodologies used in analyzing and implementing computer networks
Hafta 2: Application Layer: Socket programming, Connection-oriented and Connectionless client-server programming
Hafta 3: Transport Layer: Connection-oriented and Connectionless networking. TCP and UDP protocols
Hafta 4: Network Layer: IPv4 Addressing, Dynamic Addressing BOOTP, DHCP
Hafta 5: Network Layer: Subnetworking, ICMP, NAT
Hafta 6: Network Layer: Routing Information Exchange, Routing Algorithms
Hafta 9: Data Link Layer: Link-Layer Addressing, ARP, RARP
Hafta 10: Data Link Layer: Multiple Access Protocols
Hafta 11: Data Link Layer: Error Detection and Correction

courses.name: Cryptography and Network Security
courses.purpose: This course covers introductory level information security concepts, encryption algorithms and systems, and addresses the creation of protection mechanisms using the techniques and approaches required to ensure network security.
courses.description: Introducing literature and terminology related to cryptography and network security; providing students with information about basic encryption algorithms, systems, working principles and network security techniques.
learning_outcomes: Define the basic principles of information security and security threats.
Explain the basic principles of cryptography, different encryption and decryption algorithms.
Create simple cryptographic systems by applying cryptographic algorithms.
Explain authentication, message verification and digital signature techniques.
Identify security vulnerabilities of operating systems.
Classify types of malware and explain the precautions that can be taken against these software.
Identify threats to computer networks and determine the necessary protection mechanisms and methods to prevent these threats.
Use packet generation and network observation tools to analyze network traffic and detect potential vulnerabilities.
resources: Introduction to Computer Security. M. T. Goodrich and R. Tamassia. Pearson Prentice Hall. 2011. ISBN-13: 978-0-321-70201-2. ISBN-10: 0-321-70201-8.

courses.name: Introduction to Machine Learning
courses.purpose: Machine learning is about how to design computer programs that can automatically improve themselves with experience. The aim of this course is to review the latest and most effective algorithms used in the field of machine learning. Both theoretical properties and practical applications of these algorithms will be discussed.
courses.description: Machine learning deals with computer programs automatically improving their performance with past experience. The following topics will be covered in the machine learning course inspired by many fields such as artificial intelligence, statistics, information theory, biology and control theory; Discussion of computational learning theory, machine learning concepts, Bayesian learning, supervised learning, classification methods, regression methods, unsupervised learning, grouping methods, artificial neural networks, reinforcement learning and advanced machine learning methods.
learning_outcomes: Will be able to depict a wide range of many machine learning algorithms.
Will be able to apply basic techniques and algorithms in the field.
Will be able to compare different algorithms and techniques in the field.
Will be able to design machine learning algorithms for special situations.
Will be able to evaluate the potential areas of use of machine learning techniques.

Hafta 2: Introduction and Machine Learning Concepts
Hafta 3: Bayesian Decision Theory and Bayesian Classification
Hafta 4: Supervised Learning - Parametric Classification Methods
Hafta 5: Supervised Learning - Nonparametric Classification Methods
Hafta 11: Unsupervised Learning - Artificial Neural Networks
Hafta 12: Unsupervised Learning - Artificial Neural Networks

courses.name: Distributed Database Systems
courses.purpose: The objective of this course is to teach the students the fundamental issues in distributed systems with a strong emphasis on data management. After taking the course, students are expected to have an understanding of topics ranging from distributed transaction management and enhanced concurrency control to data replication and distributed query processing and optimization.
courses.description: Distributed database design, distributed transaction management and concurrency control, data replication, distributed query processing and optimization.
learning_outcomes: will be able to explain distributed database technology comprehensively.
will be able to describe transaction management and concurrency control in distributed database management systems.
will be able to design distributed databases when fragmentation and/or replication are required.
will be able to assess the correctness of optimistic and pessimistic concurrency control algorithms which are based on either locking or timestamp ordering.
will be able to apply distributed recovery and commit protocols in the presence of site failures and network partitioning.
weekly_topics: Hafta 6: Query Decomposition and Data Localization
resources: Ozsu; Valduriez; Principles of Distributed Database Systems; 2/e; Prentice Hall; 1999; 978-0136597070

courses.name: Special Topics in Machine Learning
courses.purpose: This course provides the mathematical and conceptual foundations for advanced machine learning methods. It covers sampling and information theory, digital filtering and the discrete Fourier transform, vector and matrix manipulations, numerical optimization, and the foundations of statistical learning theory.
courses.description: The following topics will be included in the syllabus: sampling and information theory, digital filtering and the discrete Fourier transform, basic vector and matrix operations, fundamentals of numerical optimization, fundamentals of statistical learning theory.
learning_outcomes: Be able to apply signal sampling and filtering principles by being involved in data processing algorithms.
Be able to use vectors and matrices in data processing algorithms.
Be able to use numerical optimization methods for machine learning algorithms.
Describe the principles of statistical learning theory.
Compare machine learning algorithms in terms of bias-variance trade-off, model complexity, and cross-validation.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction: What is machine learning?
Hafta 2: Signal sampling fundamentals - sampling frequency, Nyquist frequency, signal and image resolution, Shannon information theory, efficient codes, data compression
Hafta 3: Introduction to digital filtering, convolution, linear and time invariant system theory, 1D and 2D filters, linear and nonlinear filters
Hafta 4: Fourier transform, discrete Fourier transform, spectrum of signal and image, complex numbers
Hafta 5: Summary of linear algebra - row and column vectors, matrices, matrix multiplication, the exclusion product, norm
Hafta 6: Fundamentals of numerical optimization – optimality conditions, KKT conditions, slope descent optimization, convex optimization programs
Hafta 8: Primal-dual theory, large-scale optimization, stochastic gradient descent method

Hafta 9: Summary of probability, random variables and probability distributions, Bayes' theorem, expected values, Law of Large Numbers, Central Limit Theorem, Markov, Jensen, Chernoff and Hoeffding inequalities
Hafta 10: Introduction to statistical learning theory - learning as a statistical activity, supervised and unsupervised learning, regression and classification
Hafta 11: Statistical decision theory, function estimation, statistical models, restricted estimators, curse of dimensionality, bias-variance trade-off
Hafta 12: Model evaluation and selection, effective model sizes, AIC, BIC, Vapnik-Chervonenkis size
Hafta 13: Vapnik-Chervonenkis dimension, cross-validation and its properties, bootstrap methods
resources: A. Oppenheim; A. Willsky; Signals & Systems; Pearson; 1996; ISBN 0136511759
D. Lay; S. Lay; J. McDonald; Linear Algebra and Its Applications; Pearson; 5th Edition; 2015; ISBN 9780321982384
D. Bertsekas; Nonlinear Programming; Athena Scientific; 3rd Edition; 2016; ISBN 9781886529052
W. Navidi; Statistics for Engineers and Scientists; Mc-Graw Hill; 3rd Edition; 2010; ISBN 9780073376332
T. Hastie; R. Tibshirani; J. Friedman; The Elements of Statistical Learning; Springer; 2013; ISBN 9780387216065

courses.name: Distributed Systems and Parallel Computing
courses.purpose: This course will introduce the algorithms and technologies of distributed systems. It will teach both fundamentals as well as systems where these fundamentals are applied in practice. The course will be further based on advanced material from both research papers and several textbooks on distributed and parallel computing. In addition to the theoretical work, during the semester, at least one programming project will be assigned.
courses.description: To acquaint students with the major types, structures, functionality, and deployment of distributed systems, and to introduce students to the literature and terminology used for distributed systems and parallel computing.
learning_outcomes: will be able to define the structure, type, and application areas of distributed systems.
will be able to exercise infrastructure, software, hardware, languages, and operating system applications for building distributed computing environments.
will be able to classify distributed process structures (clients, servers, threads, and code migration).
will be able to describe process communication, remote procedure call, and distributed process synchronization approaches and algorithms.
will be able to discuss basic distributed applications (distributed web-based systems, distributed object-based systems, and distributed file systems).
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction: Definition and types of distributed systems
Hafta 10: Distributed objectbased systems: Architecture, processes, communication
Hafta 11: Distributed objectbased systems: naming, synchronization, consistency and replication, fault tolerance
resources: Distributed Systems Principles and Paradigms 2nd Edition Andrew Tanenbaum© 2007 | Pearson Prentice Hall | ISBN: 013239227
Distributed Computing Principles and Applications M. L. Liu ISBN10: 0201796449

courses.name: Introduction to Neural Networks
courses.purpose: This course will introduce the fundamental principles and algorithms of Artificial Neural Network (ANN) systems. The course will cover many subjects including basic neuron model, simple perceptron, adaptive linear element, Least Mean Square (LMS) algorithm, Multi Layer Perceptron (MLP), Back Propagation (BP) learning algorithm, Radial Basis Function (RBF) networks, Self Organizing Maps (SOM) and Learning Vector Quantization (LVQ), Support Vector Machines (SVMs), Continuous time and discrete time Hopfield networks, classification techniques, pattern recognition, signal processing and control applications.
courses.description: The following topics will be included in the course: The main neural network architectures and learning algorithms, perceptrons and the LMS algorithm, back propagation learning, radial basis function networks, support vector machines, Kohonen’s self organizing feature maps, Hopfield networks, artificial neural networks for signal processing, pattern recognition and control.
learning_outcomes: Describe basic artificial neural network models.
Use the most common ANN architectures and their learning algorithms for a specific application.
Explain the principles of supervised and unsupervised learning, and generalization ability.
Evaluate the practical considerations in applying ANNs to real classification, pattern recognition, signal processing and control problems.
Implement basic ANN models and algorithms using Matlab and its Neural Network Toolbox.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Biological motivation. Historical remarks on artificial neural networks. Applications of artificial neural networks. A taxonomy of artificial neural network models and learning algorithms.

Hafta 2: General artificial neuron model. Discretevalued perceptron model, threshold logic and their limitations. Discretetime (dynamical) Hopfield networks. Hebb’s rule. Connection wieght matrix as an outer product of memory patterns.

Hafta 3: Supervised learning. Perceptron learning algorithm. Adaptive linear element. Supervised learning as output error minimization problem. Gradient descent algorithm for minimization. Least mean square rule.

Hafta 4: Single layer, continuous valued perceptron. Nonlinear (sigmoidal) activation function. Delta rule. Batch mode and pattern mode gradient descent algorithms. Convergence conditions for deterministic and stochastic gradient descent algorithms.
Hafta 5: Multi layer perceptron as universal approximator. Function representation and approximation problems. Backpropagation Learning. Local minima problem. Overtraining.

Hafta 7: Batch and pattern mode training. Training set versus test set. Overfitting problem. General practices for network training and testing. Signal processing and pattern recognition applications of multilayer perceptrons.

Hafta 8: Radial Basis Function (RBF) network. Backpropagation learning for determining linear weights, centers and widths parameters of RBF networks. Random selection of centers. Input versus input-output clustering for center and width determination. Regularization theory, mixture of Gaussian (conditional probability density function) model and neurofuzzy connections of RBF networks.

Hafta 9: Parametric versus nonparametric methods for data representation. Unsupervised learning as a vector quantization problem. Competitive networks. Winner takes all networks. Kohonen’s self organizing feature map. Clustering.

Hafta 10: Signal processing applications of artificial neural networks. Principal component analysis. Data compression and reduction. Image and 1D signal compression and transformation applications of artificial neural networks.

Hafta 12: Pattern recognition applications of artificial neural networks. Artificial neural networks for feature extraction. Nonlinear feature mapping. Data fusion. Artificial neural networks as classifiers. Image and speech recognition applications.
resources: S. Haykin, Neural Networks and Learning Machines, Pearson Education, 3rd Ed., 2009, ISBN13 9780131293762 ISBN10 0131293761.
Lecture Notes.

courses.name: Music and Computers
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to provide a solid foundation in digital signal processing (DSP) and sound synthesis techniques situated at the intersection of music and computer technologies. The fundamentals of digital audio, algorithmic music production, and interactive sound systems are covered. Students learn, through hands-on practice, topics such as sampling, filtering, amplitude and frequency modulation, synthesis methods, and sound analysis. By the end of the course, students are expected to have the technical background necessary to develop their own projects in computer-assisted music production, experimental sound design and interactive performance systems.
courses.description: Fundamental concepts of acoustics, the physical and perceptual structure of sound, and the basic components of digital audio processing. Core properties of sound such as intensity (amplitude), time, and frequency and their measurements. Sound analysis techniques and methods of representation in the time and frequency domains. Sound synthesis methods and approaches to computer-based music production. Digital signal processing techniques and the processing, transformation, and structuring of sound. The representation of sound in analog and digital environments; analog-to-digital and digital-to-analog conversion processes.
learning_outcomes: Develop an original sound project focused on algorithmic and computer-based music production.
Apply digital audio processing techniques in time and frequency domains.
Explain the main principles of digital sound.
Classify the components that make up the structure of sound such as frequency, amplitude, and timbre.
Compare technical characteristics of fundamental sound synthesis methods.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Fundamental Concepts, Characteristics of sound: frequency (pitch), amplitude (loudness), spectrum (timbre)
Hafta 3: Fundamental Concepts of Acoustics: Sound analyses: pitch, rhythm, and spectrum analysis
Hafta 4: Digital Representation of Sound: digital sounds and sampling theory, storage concerns and compression, analog to digital converters (ADC), and digital to analog converters (DAC)
Hafta 5: Sound studio and recording techniques, sound editing and analysis tools
Hafta 6: General overview of computer tools and languages for generation and construction of signals, PureData intro

Hafta 8: The Frequency Domain: phasors, Fourier transformation and the sum of sine waves, DFT and FFT algorithms. Signal Modifiers: filters and phase relationships, responses and, resonance. Envelopes, gates and triggers
Hafta 10: Transformation of Sound, Effects in Time Domain: delay, reverb, localization/spatialization. Effects in Frequency Domain: phase vocoders, convolution, time stretching, pitch shifting
Hafta 11: Sound Synthesis Techniques: sampling, additive synthesis, subtractive synthesis, wave-shaping, Special synthesis algorithms
Hafta 12: Modulation Synthesis: amplitude modulation and frequency modulation
Hafta 14: Introduction to music information retrieval
resources: The Computer Music Tutorial (Technology) by Curtis Roads, ISBN-10: 0262680823, ISBN-13: 978-0262680820

courses.name: Introduction to Digital Image Processing
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental principles and algorithms of digital image processing systems. It covers the main concepts and methods used to analyze, enhance, restore, and compress digital images.
courses.description: The course content includes digital images as two-dimensional signals; two-dimensional convolution, Fourier transform, and discrete cosine transform; fundamentals of image processing; image enhancement; image restoration; image coding and compression.
learning_outcomes: Explain digital images and the sampling and quantization processes used to obtain them.
Use histogram-based techniques for digital image processing.
Apply spatial or frequency domain processing and filtering techniques to smooth and sharpen digital images.
Utilize filtering techniques to restore images in the presence of noise.
Describe morphological image processing techniques.
Explain commonly used color models and their applications in basic color image processing.
Apply digital image processing methods to practical problems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 5: Point processing, basic gray-level transformations
Hafta 6: Spatial filtering, convolution theorem, smoothing filters
Hafta 7: Spatial sharpening filters, combining spatial filtering techniques
Hafta 9: Filtering in the frequency domain, convolution theorem
Hafta 10: Image restoration for noise removal
Hafta 13: Fundamentals of image compression, JPEG image compression algorithm
resources: R. C. Gonzalez, R. E. Woods, Digital Image Processing, Pearson, 2010, 3/E, ISBN: 978-0-13-234563-7.
prerequisites: To be a junior (3th year) student

courses.name: Digital Design
courses.purpose: To teach students the fundamentals of combinational and sequential logic circuit design. To prepare students for advanced courses in microprocessors, computer architecture, and VLSI topics.
courses.description: Binary systems, representation of information, number systems and Boolean algebra, analysis and design of combinational and sequential circuits, multiplexers, decoders, comparators, adders, subtractors and counters. Simplification of Boolean functions, Karnaugh maps, properties of logic gates, timing diagrams.
learning_outcomes: Calculate conversions between different number systems and basic arithmetic operations in binary form.
Use Boolean algebra or Karnaugh maps for logic circuit optimization.
Build logic circuits using different technologies.
Design combinational logic circuits.
Design sequential logic circuits.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Number theory basics, Digital systems and information
Hafta 3: Number theory basics, Digital systems and information
Hafta 4: Combinational logic circuits: Propositional logic, Logic gates, Truth tables
Hafta 5: Combinational logic circuits: Boolean algebra identities, Duals and complements
Hafta 6: Combinational logic circuits: SOP/POS forms, Boolean function simplification via algrebraic manipulation
Hafta 7: Combinational logic circuits: Boolean function simplification via Karnaugh maps, Circuit optimization
Hafta 9: Combinational logic circuits design: Boolean function simplification via Karnaugh maps, Design procedures
Hafta 10: Combinational logic circuit design: Design procedures, Technology mapping
Hafta 11: Combinational logic circuit design: Logic functions, Decoders, Encoders, Multiplexers
Hafta 12: Combinational logic circuit design: Binary arithmetic, Radix complements, Adder circuits, Subtractors, Other arithmetic functions
Hafta 13: Sequential logic circuits: Storage elements, State tables, State diagrams as graphs/relations, FSMs
Hafta 14: Sequential logic circuits: Moore and Mealy models, Sequential circuit analysis, Design procedures
Hafta 15: Sequential logic circuits: Design procedures and examples

courses.name: Industrial Applications of Simulations
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach modelling of intermediate and advanced simulation applications and explain simulation applications for industrial applications.
courses.description: This course includes intermediate modelling applications of operations, simulation modelling of inventory systems and a small job-shop manufacturing system, experiment design and optimization, and digital twins in industry.
learning_outcomes: Model detailed manufacturing and service operations.
Analyze inventory systems with simulation models.
Perform simulation model optimization together with experimental design.
Describe the applications of digital twins in the industry.

Hafta 8: Modeling a Small Job-Shop Manufacturing System
Hafta 9: Modeling a Small Job-Shop Manufacturing System with Material Handling Tools

Hafta 13: Use cases of Digital Twins in Industry

courses.name: Stochastic Models in Manufacturing Systems
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with the ability to model uncertainties and random events in manufacturing processes; to understand the random variables and processes that affect the performance of manufacturing systems; to use appropriate mathematical modeling techniques for analyzing and improving these systems; and to interpret the results obtained using various performance metrics in order to make decisions related to system design and operations.
courses.description: Markov processes, M/M/1 queue models, transfer lines with two or more machines having different processing times, long lines, and random long lines.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to explain the role of probability theory and Markov processes in manufacturing systems.
Students will be able to use the M/M/1 queue model to model waiting times and process flows in manufacturing systems.
Students will be able to model transfer lines with fixed processing times.
Students will be able to model transfer lines with random processing times.
Students will be able to compare the performance of production lines using long line optimization and random long line models.

Hafta 5: Transfer Lines Models and Bounds

courses.name: Advanced C++: Templates and Generic Programming
courses.purpose: C++ is one of the most used programming languages in the industry thanks to its flexible design, scalability and efficiency. The main purpose of this course is to improve students' C++ knowledge and programming skills and to introduce them to the latest innovations in the C++ language. Some of these innovations are generic programming techniques, especially the Standard Template Library (STL) library, and selected Boost C++ libraries.
courses.description: In this course, students are given basic information about templates and the principles of generic programming and frequently used generic libraries are introduced.
learning_outcomes: will be able to develop programs using advanced structures within the templates, including class templates, type property classes, and rule templates.
will know the concepts of generic programming.
will be able to develop software libraries.
will know how to use graphical interface programming environments.
will know how to use the Standard Template Library and Boost-C libraries.

Hafta 2: Basic template information. Function and class templates
Hafta 3: Out-of-type template parameters, fine details

Hafta 5: Multi-format usage capabilities of templates

courses.name: Software Testing
courses.purpose: The primary objective of this course is to provide students with foundational knowledge of the principles and fundamentals of software testing. The second objective is to equip students with essential testing methods and technologies to support the development of high-quality software products. The third objective is to ensure that students acquire the necessary competencies in software testing to participate effectively in international software testing certification processes.
courses.description: It is generally accepted that it is not possible to create perfect software. It is therefore necessary to test software before it is released to the users to reduce the risk of mistakes in software production having a negative impact when the software is used. It is equally necessary to ensure that testing is performed well. This course specifies definitions and concepts, test processes, test documentation, test techniques in software engineering.
learning_outcomes: Explain test concepts and test types.
Identify the testing objectives during software development process.
Explain specific test techniques.
Identify test management concept.
Analyze case studies on software testing.
weekly_topics: Hafta 4: Testing throughout the software life cycle.
Hafta 6: Test Design Techniques: The Test Development Process.
Hafta 7: Test Design Techniques: Black Box testing techniques.
Hafta 9: Test Design Techniques: White Box testing techniques.
Hafta 10: Mutation testing, calculation of the cost of executed mutants.

courses.name: Artificial Intelligence and Expert Systems
courses.purpose: The goal of this course is to provide students with a survey of different aspects of Artificial Intelligence (AI).
courses.description: This course provides an introduction to Artificial Intelligence. It examines the theories and algorithms that constitute the fundamental components of computational intelligence.
learning_outcomes: Be able to discuss on specific domains such as computer vision, natural language processing, and robotics.
Be able to present logic as a formalism for representing knowledge in AI systems.
Be able to design AI systems that use learning to improve their performance on a given task.
By using Bayesian networks, be able to use the probability as a mechanism for handling uncertainty in AI.
Be able to apply AI search models and generic search strategies.
